name: Achieving More Value from Enterprise Applications
description: Enterprise application silos connected by inadequate integration software ('duct tape, chewing gum, and string'). If Industry Average IT ran at Best in Class cost-efficiency, $143 billion in 2006 savings would be generated; $20 billion readily achievable. SOA technologies (web services, XML, open middleware) seen by at least two-thirds of respondents as means to improve enterprise application integration. Survey covers discrete/process manufacturing, consumer, services, and government sectors. Aberdeen Competitive Framework used.
